town:Arco.text:
Arco is a comune in Trentino-Alto Adige in northern Italy.
The town is faced on one side by sheer limestone cliffs jutting up like a wall protecting it and its ancient hilltop castle.
King Francis II of the Two Sicilies died there in 1894.

town:Riva del Garda.text: Riva del Garda is a town and comune in the northern Italian province of Trento of the Trentino Alto Adige region. It is also known simply as Riva and is located at the northern tip of Lake Garda.

castle:Castello di Sabbionara D'Avio.text: The Castle of Avio is a medieval castle in the comune Avio, Trentino, northern Italy. It is currently held by the Fondo per l'Ambiente Italiano (FAI). It is one of the several castles commanding the Vallagarina valley of the Adige River. It features several rooms with medieval frescoes.

village:Bezzecca.text: Bezzecca was a comune (municipality) in Trentino in the Italian region Trentino-Alto Adige/Südtirol. On January 1, 2010 it merged in the new municipality of Ledro. It is located about 35 km southwest of Trento.

village:Brentonico.text: Brentonico is a comune (municipality) in Trentino in the northern Italian region Trentino-Alto Adige, located about 30 kilometres (19 mi) southwest of Trento. As of 31 December 2004, it had a population of 3,770 and an area of 62.6 square kilometres (24.2 mi2).

village:Limone sul Garda.text: Limone sul Garda is a town and comune in the province of Brescia, in Lombardy, at the western bank of Lake Garda.

village:Molina di Ledro.text: Molina di Ledro was a comune (municipality) in Trentino in the Italian region Trentino-Alto Adige/Südtirol. On January 1, 2010 it merged in the new municipality of Ledro. It is the most populated frazione of the municipality. It is located about 35 km southwest of Trento.

village:Pieve di Ledro.text: Pieve di Ledro was a comune (municipality) in Trentino in the Italian region Trentino-Alto Adige/Südtirol. On 1 January 2010, it merged in the new municipality of Ledro, as its municipal seat. It is located about 35 km southwest of Trento.

village:Prada.text: Brentonico is a comune (municipality) in Trentino in the northern Italian region Trentino-Alto Adige, located about 30 kilometres (19 mi) southwest of Trento. As of 31 December 2004, it had a population of 3,770 and an area of 62.6 square kilometres (24.2 mi2).

village:Ronzo-Chienis.text: Ronzo-Chienis is a comune (municipality) in Trentino in the Italian region Trentino-Alto Adige/Südtirol, located about 25 kilometres (16 mi) southwest of Trento in the Val di Gresta.

village:Sabbionara.text: The Castle of Avio is a medieval castle in the comune Avio, Trentino, northern Italy. It is currently held by the Fondo per l'Ambiente Italiano (FAI). It is one of the several castles commanding the Vallagarina valley of the Adige River. It features several rooms with medieval frescoes.

village:Tremosine sul Garda.text: Tremosine sul Garda is a comune in the Italian province of Brescia, in Lombardy, near Lake Garda. It is one of I Borghi più belli d'Italia. It is divided into 18 frazioni; Pieve is the frazione which has the town hall. Vesio is the biggest one. Campione is a frazione famous for aquatic sport, such as kitesurfing, and it is the only one on the shore of Lake Garda.

village:Varone.text: The Cascate del Varone are pair of waterfalls that fall into a vertical cave 3 kilometers north-west of the northern end of Lake Garda and the city of Riva del Garda in northern Italy. The name Varone has its origin from the town of Varone a short distance away.

peak:Monte Altissimo di Nago.text: Monte Altissimo di Nago is one of the highest summits of the Monte Baldo mountain range and thereby part of the Garda Mountains in northern Italy.

peak:Rocchetta Giochello.text: Monte Rocchetta is a 1,575 m mountain near Lake Garda, close to the city of Riva del Garda in the Trentino province of Italy.

hamlet:La Grotta.text:
Arco is a comune in Trentino-Alto Adige in northern Italy.
The town is faced on one side by sheer limestone cliffs jutting up like a wall protecting it and its ancient hilltop castle.
King Francis II of the Two Sicilies died there in 1894.

hamlet:Olzano.text: Tignale is a comune in the province of Brescia, in Lombardy, northern Italy. It is an international tourist center on the Lake Garda.

hamlet:Zucchetti.text: Tenno is a comune (municipality) in Trentino in the northern Italian region Trentino-Alto Adige/Südtirol, located about 30 kilometres (19 mi) southwest of Trento.

hamlet:Zumiani.text: Tenno is a comune (municipality) in Trentino in the northern Italian region Trentino-Alto Adige/Südtirol, located about 30 kilometres (19 mi) southwest of Trento.

castle:Castello di Arco.text: Arco Castle is a ruined castle located on a prominent spur high above Arco and the Sarca Valley in Trentino, northern Italy.

town:Mori.text: Mori is a comune (municipality) in Trentino in the northern Italian region Trentino-Alto Adige/Südtirol, located about 25 kilometres (16 mi) southwest of Trento.

village:Ala.text: Ala is a comune in Trentino, north-eastern Italy. Until the First World War, it was an important border town between the Kingdom of Italy and the Austro Hungarian Empire.

village:Isera.text: Isera is a comune (municipality) in Trentino in the northern Italian region Trentino-Alto Adige/Südtirol, located about 20 kilometres (12 mi) southwest of Trento. As of 31 December 2004, it had a population of 2,496 and an area of 14.1 square kilometres (5.4 mi2).

village:Avio.text: Avio is a comune in Trentino in north Italy. It is about 50 kilometres (31 mi) from Trento, in the Vallagarina, and is crossed by the Adige river. Avio occupies a flat plain, bounded by the Monte Baldo on the east and by the Monti Lessini on the west.

castle:Castello di Castellano.text: The Castle of Castellano is a manor house in the village of Castellano, in the municipality of Villa Lagarina, in Trentino, northern Italy.

village:Castellano.text: Castellano is a little village which is a frazione of the municipality of Villa Lagarina in Trentino. It was the most important village of the Feudo of Castellano and during the Austria-Hungary period was the site of the Comune di Castellano, abolished in 1929.

village:Tiarno di Sotto.text: Tiarno di Sotto was a comune (municipality) in the Trentino in the Italian region Trentino-Alto Adige/Südtirol. On January 1, 2010 it merged in the new municipality of Ledro. It is located about 40 km southwest of Trento.

village:Tignale.text: Tignale is a comune in the province of Brescia, in Lombardy, northern Italy. It is an international tourist center on the Lake Garda.

peak:Cima Prà della Baziva.text: Monte Baldo is a mountain range in the Italian Alps, located in the provinces of Trento and Verona. Its ridge is orientated in a northeast-southwest direction and it is bounded to the south by the highland ending at Caprino Veronese, to the west by Lake Garda, to the north by the valley joining Rovereto to Nago-Torbole and, to the east, the Val d'Adige.

peak:Monte Cadria.text: Monte Cadria is a mountain in the Alps located in Italy. It is the highest peak of the Brescia and Garda Prealps.

peak:Monte Stivo.text: Monte Stivo is a 2,059 m mountain near Lake Garda, close to the cities of Arco and Riva del Garda in Italy.

peak:Monte Tremalzo.text: Monte Tremalzo is a mountain between Lombardy and Trentino Alto Adige, Italy.

hamlet:Tremalzo.text: The Tremalzo Pass (el. 1702 m.) is a mountain pass in Trentino in Italy.

city:Verona.text: Verona is the largest city in the region of Veneto in Italy, with 255,131 inhabitants. It is one of the seven provincial capitals of the region, and is the largest city municipality in the region and in northeastern Italy. The metropolitan area of Verona covers an area of 1,426 km2 (550.58 sq mi) and has a population of 714,310 inhabitants.

town:Bussolengo.text: Bussolengo is an Italian town of 20,955 inhabitants on the Adige River in province of Verona, Veneto. One of its oldest churches is the frescoed church of San Valentino.

town:Desenzano del Garda.text: Desenzano del Garda is a town and comune in the province of Brescia, in Lombardy, Italy, on the southwestern shore of Lake Garda. It borders the communes of Castiglione delle Stiviere, Lonato, Padenghe sul Garda, and Sirmione.

town:Negrar di Valpolicella.text: Negrar di Valpolicella is a comune (municipality) in the Province of Verona in the Italian region Veneto, about 110 kilometres (68 mi) west of Venice and about 12 kilometres (7 mi) northwest of Verona. Since 8 February 2019, the official name has been changed to "Negrar di Valpolicella" after a referendum was held in the town.

town:Pescantina.text: Pescantina is a comune (municipality) in the Province of Verona in the Italian region Veneto, located about 110 kilometres (68 mi) west of Venice and about 11 kilometres (7 mi) northwest of Verona. As of 31 December 2004, it had a population of 14,096 and an area of 19.7 square kilometres (7.6 mi2).

town:Peschiera del Garda.text: Peschiera del Garda is a town and comune in the province of Verona, in Veneto, Italy. When Lombardy-Venetia was under Austrian rule, Peschiera was the northwest anchor of the four fortified towns constituting the Quadrilatero. The fortress is on an island in the river Mincio at its outlet from Lake Garda.

town:Rovereto.text: Rovereto is a city and comune in Trentino in northern Italy, located in the Vallagarina valley of the Adige River.

town:San Pietro in Cariano.text: San Pietro in Cariano is a comune (municipality) in the Province of Verona in the Italian region Veneto, located about 110 kilometres (68 mi) west of Venice and about 12 kilometres (7 mi) northwest of Verona. It is located in the geographical region of Valpolicella.

town:Sant'Ambrogio di Valpolicella.text: Sant'Ambrogio di Valpolicella is a comune (municipality) in the Province of Verona in the Italian region Veneto, located about 120 kilometres west of Venice and about 15 km (9 mi) northwest of Verona.

town:Sirmione.text: Sirmione is a comune in the province of Brescia, in Lombardy. It is bounded by Desenzano del Garda (Lombardy) and Peschiera del Garda in the province of Verona and the region of Veneto. It has a historical centre which is located on the Sirmio peninsula that divides the lower part of Lake Garda.

castle:Castelvecchio.text: Castelvecchio is a castle in Verona, northern Italy. It is the most important military construction of the Scaliger dynasty that ruled the city in the Middle Ages.

castle:Vecchio castello d'ingresso.text: Gardaland Resort is an amusement park resort in Castelnuovo del Garda, Province of Verona, Italy. Opened on 19 July 1975, the resort includes Gardaland Park, Gardaland Sea-Life, Legoland Waterpark, Gardaland Hotel, Adventure Hotel and Magic Hotel. It is adjacent to Lake Garda. The entire complex covers an area of 520,000 m2 (5,597,233 sq ft), while the amusement park alone measures 200,000 m2 (2,152,782 sq ft).

village:Affi.text: Affi is a comune (municipality) in the Province of Verona in the Italian region Veneto, located about 120 kilometres (75 mi) west of Venice and about 20 kilometres (12 mi) northwest of Verona.

village:Bardolino.text: Bardolino is a comune (municipality) in the Province of Verona in the Italian region Veneto, located about 130 kilometres (81 mi) west of Venice and about 25 kilometres (16 mi) northwest of Verona.

village:Caprino Veronese.text: Caprino Veronese is a comune (municipality) in the Province of Verona in the Italian region Veneto, located about 120 kilometres (75 mi) west of Venice and about 30 kilometres (19 mi) northwest of Verona.

village:Castelletto.text: Brenzone sul Garda is a comune (municipality) in the Province of Verona in the Italian region of Veneto, located on the eastern shore of Lake Garda about 120 kilometres west of Venice and about 35 km (22 mi) northwest of Verona.

village:Castelnuovo del Garda.text: Castelnuovo del Garda is an Italian comune (municipality), in the Province of Verona, in Veneto, on a couple of morainic hills a few kilometres south-east of Lake Garda. Verona is about 20 km to the east, Venice is 140 km east and Milan 140 km west.

village:Costermano sul Garda.text: Costermano sul Garda is a comune (municipality) in the Province of Verona in the Italian region Veneto, located about 120 kilometres (75 mi) west of Venice and about 25 kilometres (16 mi) northwest of Verona.

village:Dolcè.text: Dolcè is a comune (municipality) in the Province of Verona in the Italian region Veneto, located about 120 kilometres (75 mi) west of Venice and about 20 kilometres (12 mi) northwest of Verona, in the Adige Valley.

village:Gardone Riviera.text: Gardone Riviera is a town and comune in the province of Brescia, in Lombardy. It is situated on the western shore of Lake Garda. It is one of I Borghi più belli d'Italia.

village:Lazise.text: Lazise is a comune (municipality) and town in the Province of Verona in the Italian region Veneto, located about 120 kilometres west of Venice and about 20 kilometres northwest of Verona. It is situated on the eastern shore of Lake Garda. As of 31 December 2004, it had a population of 6,213 and an area of 65.0 square kilometres (25.1 sq mi).

village:Manerba del Garda.text: Manerba del Garda is a town and comune in the province of Brescia, in Lombardy. It is located at the southwest side of the Lake Garda.

village:Marniga.text: Brenzone sul Garda is a comune (municipality) in the Province of Verona in the Italian region of Veneto, located on the eastern shore of Lake Garda about 120 kilometres west of Venice and about 35 km (22 mi) northwest of Verona.

village:Moniga del Garda.text: Moniga del Garda is a town and comune in the province of Brescia, in the Italian region Lombardy. The municipality, located on the southwest side of Lake Garda, is bounded by other communes of Padenghe sul Garda, Manerba del Garda and Soiano del Lago.

village:Montinelle.text: Manerba del Garda is a town and comune in the province of Brescia, in Lombardy. It is located at the southwest side of the Lake Garda.

village:Morgnaga.text: Gardone Riviera is a town and comune in the province of Brescia, in Lombardy. It is situated on the western shore of Lake Garda. It is one of I Borghi più belli d'Italia.

village:Padenghe sul Garda.text: Padenghe sul Garda is a town and comune in the province of Brescia, in Lombardy. It is situated on the southwest coast of Lake Garda. Neighbouring communes are Calvagese della Riviera, Desenzano del Garda, Lonato del Garda, Moniga del Garda and Soiano del Lago.

village:Pai di Sopra.text: Torri del Benaco is a comune (municipality) in the Province of Verona in the Italian region Veneto, located about 130 kilometres west of Venice and about 30 km (19 mi) northwest of Verona, on the eastern coast of the Lake Garda.

village:Pastrengo.text: Pastrengo is a comune (municipality) in the Province of Verona in the Italian region Veneto, located about 120 kilometres (75 mi) west of Venice and about 15 kilometres (9 mi) northwest of Verona. As of 31 December 2004, it had a population of 2,486 and an area of 9.0 square kilometres (3.5 mi2).

village:Pazzon.text: Caprino Veronese is a comune (municipality) in the Province of Verona in the Italian region Veneto, located about 120 kilometres (75 mi) west of Venice and about 30 kilometres (19 mi) northwest of Verona.

village:Pesina.text: Caprino Veronese is a comune (municipality) in the Province of Verona in the Italian region Veneto, located about 120 kilometres (75 mi) west of Venice and about 30 kilometres (19 mi) northwest of Verona.

village:Polpenazze del Garda.text: Polpenazze del Garda is a comune in the province of Brescia, in Lombardy, northern Italy. It is situated near the western shore of Lake Garda.

village:Puegnago del Garda.text: Puegnago del Garda is a comune in the province of Brescia, in Lombardy.

village:Rivoli Veronese.text: Rivoli Veronese is a little town (comune) in the Province of Verona, Veneto, Italy, located on the hills overlooking the right bank of the river Adige, 20 kilometres (12 mi) northwest of Verona.

village:Rubiana.text: Caprino Veronese is a comune (municipality) in the Province of Verona in the Italian region Veneto, located about 120 kilometres (75 mi) west of Venice and about 30 kilometres (19 mi) northwest of Verona.

village:San Zeno di Montagna.text: San Zeno di Montagna is a comune (municipality) in the Province of Verona in the Italian region Veneto, located about 130 kilometres west of Venice and about 30 km (19 mi) northwest of Verona.

village:Soiano del Lago.text: Soiano del Lago is a comune in the province of Brescia, in Lombardy.

village:Spiazzi.text: The Sanctuary of Our Lady of the Crown is a shrine in Ferrara di Monte Baldo, Province of Verona, northern Italy. It is a 16th-century church built amidst the territories of the municipalities of Ferrara di Monte Baldo and Spiazzi, frazione of Caprino Veronese, in the province of Verona, Italy.

village:Torri del Benaco.text: Torri del Benaco is a comune (municipality) in the Province of Verona in the Italian region Veneto, located about 130 kilometres west of Venice and about 30 km (19 mi) northwest of Verona, on the eastern coast of the Lake Garda.

village:Toscolano Maderno.text: Toscolano Maderno is a town and comune on the West coast of Lake Garda, in the province of Brescia, in the region of Lombardy, northern Italy. It is located about 40 kilometres (25 mi) from Brescia.

peak:Dosso dei Cavalli.text: Monte Baldo is a mountain range in the Italian Alps, located in the provinces of Trento and Verona. Its ridge is orientated in a northeast-southwest direction and it is bounded to the south by the highland ending at Caprino Veronese, to the west by Lake Garda, to the north by the valley joining Rovereto to Nago-Torbole and, to the east, the Val d'Adige.

peak:Monte Pizzocolo.text: Monte Pizzocolo is a mountain of Lombardy, Italy. It has an elevation of 1,581 metres.

peak:Sasso.text: Manerba del Garda is a town and comune in the province of Brescia, in Lombardy. It is located at the southwest side of the Lake Garda.

hamlet:Bornico.text: Giuseppe Zanardelli was an Italian jurist and political figure. He served as the Prime Minister of Italy from 15 February 1901 to 3 November 1903. An eloquent orator, he was also a Grand Master freemason. Zanardelli, representing the bourgeoisie from Lombardy, personified the classical 19th-century liberalism, committed to suffrage expansion, anticlericalism, civil liberties, free trade and laissez-faire economics.

hamlet:Incaffi.text: Affi is a comune (municipality) in the Province of Verona in the Italian region Veneto, located about 120 kilometres (75 mi) west of Venice and about 20 kilometres (12 mi) northwest of Verona.

hamlet:Maclino.text: Marcus Nonius Macrinus was a Roman senator and general during the reigns of the Emperors Antoninus Pius, Lucius Verus, and Marcus Aurelius. Macrinus was suffect consul in the nundinium of April–June 154 as the colleague of [Prifernius ?] Paetus.
